summary: Implemented M2 economy/population scaffolding in sim-core: optional explicit state.m2 authority block for M2 worlds with population groups, agricultural district phases, labor commitments, grain/cash stock, and minimal market cash/grain flow state. Added sim.commit-labor GameCommand v1 and sim.list-m2-economy-summaries query so player and AI actors use the same preview/submit validation and apply path. Scheduler daily authority step now includes deterministic M2 economy/population progression. Labor commitments expire by GameDay, available labor is recomputed, agriculture phases advance by integer calendar months, and harvest settlement updates grain stock. M2 runtime content boot now initializes state.m2 for the 30 District/10 Settlement fixture. Existing M1/minimal fixture worlds remain without state.m2 for old hash/save compatibility.

tests_run.result: Initial TDD run failed as expected: 6 M2 tests failed because state.m2, sim.commit-labor, and sim.list-m2-economy-summaries did not exist.

tests_run.command: pnpm vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/sim-core-m2-economy-population.test.ts tests/protocol-command-query-schema.test.ts tests/sim-core-scheduler.test.ts tests/sim-core-boot-boundary.test.ts

tests_run.result: Test Files 8 passed; Tests 41 passed. Includes tests/sim-core-m2-economy-population.test.ts property/invariant coverage.
Node/Worker-compatible hello hash 61e0ba1c matched; command/query hash d275faa4 matched; save/load hash 28074edd matched.
Architecture boundaries passed; sim determinism matched; root Vitest Test Files 15 passed, Tests 75 passed; web Test Files 1 passed, Tests 3 passed.
Prettier, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture check passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M2-ECON-POP-001

tests_run.result: Shows implementation changes in packages/protocol, packages/sim-core, tests, existing orchestration state files from lead thread, existing route message, and this handoff file.

decisions: M2 authority state is optional on WorldRuntimeStateV0 for compatibility. M2 content boot worlds include explicit state.m2 fields and those fields are included in canonical hash; M1/minimal worlds omit state.m2 and keep existing M1 hashes.
PopulationGroupId is a branded numeric id derived deterministically from district id for the M2 scaffold.
Agricultural phases are deterministic integer calendar phases: months 3-4 planting, 5-8 growing, 9 harvest, all other months fallow.

sim.commit-labor currently supports purpose mobilized only. It reserves labor for durationDays, records cumulative mobilization cost in market cashFlow, and does not implement full taxation, war manpower, diplomacy, vassal politics, or balancing.
Scheduler system order now includes scheduler.m2-economy-population at order 250 with systemId tie-breaker; this is an R3 scheduler/protocol change routed to systems_architect.
risks: packages/save-format was outside the allowed paths, so no M2 save schema expansion was made. Existing M1 save/load canaries still pass; architect should decide whether a follow-up R3 save-format task is required before M2 exit for M2 snapshot persistence.
M2 economy formulas are scaffold constants for proof of linkage only, not balance values.
M2 state hash for runtime content boot changed from 42e3a2fe to d280a188 because new authoritative M2 fields are now included in canonical state hash.
requested_action: Review R3 scheduler/protocol/world-state changes for architecture acceptance, especially optional state.m2 compatibility and the deferred save-format decision.
